questions.question: The locusts from the abyss were told not to harm what?
options: The grass or any plant or tree, only people without God's seal
questions.correct: The grass or any plant or tree, only people without God's seal
questions.feedback: The locusts could only harm people who did not have the seal of God.

questions.question: The king over the locusts from the abyss is named Abaddon in Hebrew. What is his Greek name?
questions.feedback: The king of the abyss is Abaddon in Hebrew, Apollyon in Greek — meaning Destroyer.

questions.question: The rest of mankind who survived these plagues repented of their sins.
questions.feedback: The rest of mankind still did not repent of their idolatry, murders, or sorcery.

questions.question: The locusts were given power like the power of _____ of the earth.
questions.feedback: The locusts were given power like scorpions of the earth.

questions.question: The four angels bound at the great river _____ were released to kill a third of mankind.
questions.feedback: The four angels at the Euphrates were released.
